title: Dvd brightness level
wikitext:
[]: Dvd brightness level             Quote Post by x25 » Tue Oct 28, 2008 1:19 am When I watched the DVD release of rebuild I noticed that the picture was darker than the theatrical trailer was. I know that the DVD release was optimized for DVD but why did they mess around with the brightness level? Some of the night time scenes lose detail and are harder to see.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Tue Oct 28, 2008 3:57 am Turn the brightness up on your telly..... Fixed

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Tue Oct 28, 2008 1:54 pm i noticed that too. i watch the trailer and then the movie on my pc and i can see a gigantic difference. especially in the sachiel fight

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Oct 30, 2008 8:51 am Dartz wrote: Turn the brightness up on your telly.....

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Thu Oct 30, 2008 11:56 am Yeah. The image quality is severely darkened for the DVD release and it was a move made that makes no sense to me. Shame. But it's a better quality image than the horrendous camrips I had to settle with a few months ago. Course I'm always hoping for that magical surprise when a festival near me might play a screening of it on film. I just missed it in Germany last summer. Side note- anyone know if the German DVD the same dark image quality? A yes or no will suffice.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Thu Oct 30, 2008 10:46 pm So has anyone come up with an explanation for the brightness shift? It seems rather dumb to me to essentially rape the hard work of the animators.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Thu Oct 30, 2008 11:04 pm Could be a lot of things. I have films like Dark City that have similarly suffered, so maybe it's an encoder issue. MPEG2 is not a fantastic codec by modern standards, especially for animation. Unfortunately, DVD is as good as it gets until someone creates a good alternative to Blu-Ray that is affordable for low-volume releases, or they drop AACS licensing .

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Fri Oct 31, 2008 1:41 pm if only it came with a digital copy....

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Oct 31, 2008 6:23 pm Axell wrote: if only it came with a digital copy....

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 04, 2008 12:13 am Thing is, it's not just darker. The image quality itself is awfully crappy considering Eva 1.0 is a digital work -- we should be getting brilliantly clean lines and colors, and the DVD is everything but. Interestingly, the image bounces, as well; stare at the opening title set against black to see what I mean. Why would footage from a digitally-produced film bounce?? (The footage in the trailers on disc 2 doesn't do this, BTW.)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Nov 04, 2008 1:16 pm I'm hoping these mistakes will be rectified for any inevitable future releases. Either way, it's a shame that the 1.01 release has this problem since it supposedly has improved visuals over the original 1.0 release.... Speaking of which, does the regular edition 1.0 release also have a problem with the brightness? Or is it only a Limited Edition problem?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Nov 05, 2008 9:42 am Reichu wrote: Why would footage from a digitally-produced film bounce?

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Wed Nov 05, 2008 11:50 am Just received my german dvd now, and I don't think that the image is "too dark". And even if it happens to be dark, I think it increases the dramatic effect. So, no problem for me. I can't really compare, I erased the screener divx I had, the quality was so awful that I never had the motivation to really watch it.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Wed Nov 05, 2008 2:54 pm compare the first fight between your dvd and the trailer. thats how you can tell and whats so bad about digital copy? (keep in mind i actually have never gotten a dvd with a digital copy i just thought the idea was good)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Nov 05, 2008 3:08 pm Jinroh wrote: And even if it happens to be dark, I think it increases the dramatic effect.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Wed Nov 05, 2008 5:46 pm It does! >:- 0 I'll make comparison shots with the trailer then. edit: Ok, so actually, I find the trailer shots way too bright. I don't think it was meant to look like that. edit 2: But you might be right about the loss of details. I wonder if it's intentional or not. Makes me think about the AvP2 dvd, it's so dark that you can barely see anything, unlike the blu-ray version. Well, anyway, some parts are way too dark, while others are just fine. I don't really understand why they did that.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Nov 05, 2008 7:08 pm Jinroh wrote: Ok, so actually, I find the trailer shots way too bright.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Thu Nov 06, 2008 12:51 am Reichu wrote: Thing is, it's not just darker. The image quality itself is awfully crappy considering Eva 1.0 is a digital work -- we should be getting brilliantly clean lines and colors, and the DVD is everything but.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Thu Nov 06, 2008 1:33 am I'm gonna go with it was encoder issues. Best Suggestion, wait for Bluray, I guess. As for image clarity, I'm reserving my judgment until I see something that is not from a downloaded file, but a direct screen grab from the DVD in a lossless format (like PNG) or have the DVD in my hands.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Thu Nov 06, 2008 5:37 am I can do that anytime, if you want shots in PNG, just ask. ok, two shots, same kaworu shots, and the credits. The image is FAR from sharp. But maybe I'm just getting used to HD movies... kaworu: http://farm4.static.flickr.com/3224/3007078953_c3eb78a2eb_b_d.jpg credits: http://farm4.static.flickr.com/3073/3007913630_92ce298502_b_d.jpg edit: Nooo! That damn flickr changed the format and resized the pictures. Does anyone know where I could upload easily pictures in their original quality?

I know that the DVD release was optimized for DVD but why did they mess around with the brightness level? Some of the night time scenes lose detail and are harder to see.
